fundamentals.name: Bloom Energy Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Bloom Energy Corporation designs, manufactures, sells, and installs solid oxide fuel cell systems for on-site power generation in the United States and internationally. It offers Bloom Energy Server, an energy server platform to convert fuel, such as natural gas, biogas, hydrogen, or a blend of these fuels, into electricity through a non-combustion electrochemical process. The company also provides Bloom Electrolyzer for producing hydrogen. It sells its products through direct and indirect sales channels to utilities, data centers, retail, healthcare, education, telecom, manufacturing, and other industries. The company was formerly known as Ion America Corp. and changed its name to Bloom Energy Corporation in 2006. Bloom Energy Corporation was incorporated in 2001 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing company with a substantial new deal, but faces high valuation and mixed profitability.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 242.4% (EPS 0.44 vs 0.13); next earnings date is 2026-04-28.
positives: Revenue growth 130.4% YoY — exceptionally strong top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current FY 179.8% — significant future growth expected.
Last quarter EPS surprise of 242.4% (0.44 vs 0.13) indicates strong earnings beat.
Current ratio 5.03 and Quick ratio 3.98 suggest excellent short-term liquidity.
Free cash flow (TTM) $265.52M indicates positive cash generation.
negatives: Forward P/E 67.97x — valuation is very high.
Price/Sales (TTM) 34.12x — also indicates a high valuation.
Profit margin 0.2% and Return on equity 1.3% — profitability is very thin.
Mean analyst target of $260.18 is below the current spot price.

news.paragraph: Bloom Energy recently secured a significant $2.6 billion deal with Nebius AI, a development that has buoyed the stock and outpaced its industry peers over the past six months. This follows positive commentary from Jim Cramer, who called the stock a "rocket ship." While the company's revenue growth is exceptionally strong, its profitability metrics and high valuation warrant careful consideration.
